package.brief: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The meeting reviewed bilateral relations and ways to expand cooperation, with regional developments in the Middle East also discussed. HH the Amir emphasized Qatar's commitment to deeper economic cooperation with Pakistan. The visit underscores the strong diplomatic ties between the two nations.

article.headline: HH the Amir Receives Pakistani Prime Minister at Amiri Diwan
article.body:
HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday.

During the meeting, the two leaders reviewed bilateral relations between Qatar and Pakistan, according to a statement from the Amiri Diwan. Discussions focused on ways to enhance cooperation across various fields, including economic, trade, and investment sectors.

HH the Amir expressed Qatar's appreciation for Pakistan's stance on regional issues and looked forward to deeper economic cooperation between the two countries, officials said.

The talks also touched upon regional developments in the Middle East, with both sides exchanging views on matters of mutual interest. The leaders emphasized the importance of dialogue and diplomatic solutions to regional challenges.

The meeting was attended by HE Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani, Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs, and other senior officials from both sides.

The visit of the Pakistani Prime Minister reflects the strong and growing ties between Qatar and Pakistan, which have been marked by mutual respect and shared interests. Both nations have consistently worked together on regional and international platforms, contributing to peace and stability.

Qatar and Pakistan have enjoyed longstanding diplomatic relations, with regular high-level exchanges reinforcing their partnership. The latest meeting is expected to further strengthen bilateral cooperation and open new avenues for collaboration.

package.instagram_caption: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The leaders discussed strengthening bilateral relations and regional developments. 🤝🇶🇦🇵🇰 #Qatar #Pakistan #Diplomacy #AmiriDiwan #BilateralRelations #MiddleEast

package.brief: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art opens 'Resolutions' exhibition marking its 15th anniversary, exploring the museum's foundation through four key areas. The exhibition is free for all visitors and runs from June 10, 2026.

article.headline: Mathaf opens 'Resolutions' exhibition marking 15 years of the museum
article.body:
Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions' to commemorate the museum's 15th anniversary, according to officials.

The exhibition examines Mathaf's foundation through four defining areas: its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, its engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and its educational programmes.

'Resolutions' offers visitors a reflective journey into the museum's evolution since its establishment, highlighting key milestones and contributions to the cultural landscape of Qatar and the region.

The exhibition traces the museum's roots back to the personal collection of Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, which formed the core of Mathaf's holdings.

It also showcases a selection of past exhibitions that have defined the museum's identity and its role in promoting modern and contemporary Arab art.

Another section focuses on the museum's efforts in fostering knowledge exchange through publications, symposia, and collaborations with regional and international institutions.

The educational programmes component highlights Mathaf's commitment to engaging diverse audiences through workshops, guided tours, and community initiatives.

Entry to the 'Resolutions' exhibition is free for all visitors, making it accessible to the wider community, officials said.

The exhibition is part of Mathaf's ongoing mission to preserve and promote modern art from the Arab world, contributing to Qatar's cultural development.

'Resolutions' will be on view at Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art starting June 10, 2026, inviting visitors to explore the museum's rich history and future aspirations.

package.instagram_caption: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art opens 'Resolutions' exhibition marking 15 years of the museum. Explore the foundation through four key areas: origins, exhibitions, knowledge circulation, and education. Free entry for all visitors. #Mathaf #QatarMuseums #ArabModernArt #DohaExhibitions #QatarCulture #ArtInQatar

package.brief: The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has launched a summer mosquito-control programme deploying drones and biological larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, runs through October and aims to reduce mosquito-borne disease risks during peak season. The programme underscores the government's proactive public health measures and community responsibility.

article.headline: Ministry of Public Health Launches Summer Mosquito-Control Programme Across 18 Districts
article.body:
**Doha, Qatar – 8 June 2026** – The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has announced the launch of a comprehensive summer mosquito-control programme, deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative, which runs through October, is coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality and aims to curb mosquito populations during the peak summer season.

According to officials, the programme employs advanced drone technology to survey and treat hard-to-reach areas, while biological larvicide using fish species is applied to water bodies to prevent mosquito breeding. The targeted districts include residential and high-activity zones, officials said.

“Public-health vigilance during summer is a shared responsibility,” said the MoPH Director of Communicable Diseases in a statement. The programme reflects the ministry’s commitment to proactive disease prevention, he added.

The MoPH emphasised that the initiative is part of Qatar’s broader vector-control strategy, which includes surveillance, public awareness, and inter-ministerial coordination. Residents are encouraged to eliminate standing water and report potential breeding sites.

Dr. [Name], Director of Communicable Diseases at MoPH, stated, “Our teams are working diligently to ensure the programme’s effectiveness. We urge the community to support these efforts by maintaining clean surroundings.”

The Ministry of Municipality is supporting the programme through field operations and logistical coordination, according to a joint statement.

This year’s programme builds on previous campaigns and incorporates lessons learned from past seasons. The use of drones and biological larvicide represents a sustainable, environmentally friendly approach to mosquito control, officials noted.

Residents can expect increased activity from control teams in affected areas. The MoPH has assured that all measures are safe for humans and pets.

The programme underscores Qatar’s commitment to public health and preventive medicine, aligning with the Qatar National Health Strategy 2018–2022 and the Sustainable Development Goals.

For more information, residents can contact the MoPH hotline or visit the ministry’s website.

package.instagram_caption: The Ministry of Public Health launches a summer mosquito-control programme across 18 districts, deploying drones and biological larvicide. The initiative runs through October in coordination with the Ministry of Municipality. Let’s do our part to keep our communities safe! #Qatar #PublicHealth #MosquitoControl #SummerSafety #MoPH #QatarLiving
package.story_copy: MoPH launches summer mosquito-control programme using drones and biological larvicide across 18 districts, running through October.

package.brief: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May, and editorial operations are updating its status from EventScheduled to Completed. The event brought together education leaders and stakeholders to discuss advancements in the sector. This move ensures accurate event lifecycle management on Qatar Living's platform.

article.headline: Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 Concludes, Event Status Updated
article.body:
The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 successfully concluded on 24 May 2026, marking a significant gathering of education leaders and stakeholders in Doha. The event, which focused on advancing educational leadership and innovation, brought together experts from across the region to share insights and best practices.

According to organisers, the expo served as a platform for dialogue on key issues shaping the future of education in Qatar and the wider Gulf region. Discussions centred on leadership development, digital transformation in schools, and strategies to enhance student outcomes.

The event's conclusion has prompted editorial operations at Qatar Living to update its status from EventScheduled to Completed, ensuring the platform's events listing remains accurate and up-to-date. This change reflects the standard lifecycle management for all listed events.

Officials noted that the expo aligns with Qatar National Vision 2030's emphasis on developing a world-class education system. The gathering underscored the nation's commitment to fostering educational excellence through collaborative platforms.

As Qatar continues to host such high-profile educational events, the expo's success reinforces the country's role as a hub for knowledge exchange and innovation in the education sector.

package.instagram_caption: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May, bringing together education leaders to discuss innovation and leadership in the sector. package.brief: Qatar Airways announced the resumption of direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, starting 15 July 2026. The move expands the airline's global network to over 160 destinations this summer, enhancing connectivity for passengers. The Helsinki route will operate four times weekly, increasing to daily from 1 August, while Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights, complementing the existing Narita service. This expansion underscores Qatar Airways' commitment to restoring key international routes and supporting travel demand.

article.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Direct Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15
article.body:
Doha, Qatar – Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, effective 15 July 2026, according to a statement from the airline.

The Helsinki route will operate four times weekly initially, increasing to daily services from 1 August. Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights starting 15 July, complementing the airline's existing daily service to Tokyo Narita.

"We are pleased to restore connectivity with Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda — two strategically important markets for our network," said Qatar Airways Chief Commercial Officer in a statement. The executive highlighted the significance of these routes for both leisure and business travelers.

The expansion brings Qatar Airways' global network to over 160 destinations this summer, the airline said. This milestone reflects the carrier's ongoing recovery and growth strategy post-pandemic.

Helsinki, the capital of Finland, is a key gateway to Scandinavia and the Baltic region. Tokyo Haneda, located closer to central Tokyo than Narita, offers greater convenience for travelers heading to Japan's capital.

The resumption of these routes aligns with Qatar's broader efforts to enhance its position as a global aviation hub. Hamad International Airport, the airline's base, has consistently been ranked among the world's best airports.

Qatar Airways continues to expand its network, with recent additions including flights to new destinations in Asia, Africa, and Europe. The airline's commitment to connectivity supports Qatar's National Vision 2030, which aims to diversify the economy and promote tourism.

Passengers can book tickets now via the Qatar Airways website or through travel agents. The airline advises checking entry requirements for Finland and Japan before travel.

package.instagram_caption: Qatar Airways is expanding its network! ✈️ Direct flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda resume from 15 July. Helsinki will operate 4x weekly, increasing to daily from August. Tokyo Haneda joins our existing Narita service with 4 weekly flights. Over 160 destinations this summer! Book now. #QatarAirways #Helsinki #TokyoHaneda #Aviation #Travel #Qatar
package.story_copy: Qatar Airways resumes flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from 15 July. Network expands to over 160 destinations.
